purpose: Machine-readable requirements for current commercial Console objects.
machineBoundary: Current commercial business objects, route ownership, required route/action capabilities, and evidence requirements.

lifecycle.removalCondition: Replace only with a newer current contract for the same boundary.

productTruth: OPL Console / OPL Cloud Console commercial launch slice
currentSlice: Invite-only owner login, live Sub2API Wallet/Key/Usage facts, multiple independent Workspace subscriptions, live Fabric resource details, Runtime readiness, and Ledger receipts.
principles: Active business object contract contains only current commercial objects.
Workspace is the stable commercial object: stable URL, access state, lifecycle, persistent StorageVolume, and current runtime pointer.

Ordinary Runtime status is non-secret; only the Workspace owner user may reveal or rotate the Runtime password through private, no-store commands, and passwords never enter the persisted Workspace projection, operation payloads, audit, logs, or Ledger.
ComputeAllocation is one replaceable Workspace-owned dedicated CVM or Kubernetes node selected from the package catalog.
StorageVolume can exist without compute; StorageAttachment mounts one volume to one current ComputeAllocation runtime.
RuntimeTemplate/ImageRef is deployable runtime configuration only; it is not a billing object, storage owner, or Workspace identity.
At unpaid expiry Control Plane denies Workspace access and performs no Fabric or Tencent mutation; Tencent expiry policy owns eventual CVM/CBS reclamation and no customer resume route exists.
Destroying storage makes the Workspace unrecoverable because the file body is gone.
Sub2API owns the only spendable balance, API keys, AI usage, and real-time API debits.
Sub2API owns customer credential authentication; Control Plane stores only the verified mapping and local Session.
Control Plane owns the account-to-Sub2API mapping, monthly charge operations, price snapshots, entitlement, renewal, and expiry policy.
Workspace is the only customer monthly charge owner; new ComputeAllocation and StorageVolume rows are provider fulfillment facts without customer billing fields.
Ledger owns append-only evidence receipts, review evidence, and reconciliation reports; it does not own a spendable balance.

Fabric owns live ComputeAllocation, StorageVolume, StorageAttachment, Workspace Runtime, provider execution evidence, and FabricOperation facts; unavailable live readback never falls back to a Control Plane status copy.
Console presents Account, Session, Workspace lifecycle, AdminAuditEvent, SupportTicketMapping, and product DTOs; it calls only Control Plane product APIs.
Frontend displays backend DTOs and must not derive pricing, billing, lifecycle, or provider state locally.

Backup, recovery, sync, transfer, payment orders, and raw provider diagnostics are forbidden Pilot customer surfaces; owned general Key mutation is projected through Control Plane with delegated user credentials.
repoBoundaryRules: Console owns UI and product presentation through Control Plane APIs only; Sub2API authenticates customer credentials.
Fabric owns compute, storage, attachment, Workspace runtime, and provider execution boundaries.
Ledger owns evidence, audit, reconciliation, and review policy boundaries.

objectKinds.boundary: Ownership account for users, resources, Sub2API identity mapping, workspaces, audit, and support mappings.
Login user bound to an Account; deletion revokes sessions and preserves audit/resource evidence.
Authenticated Console session scoped to a user and account.
Workspace-owned dedicated CVM or Kubernetes node allocation selected from the package compute catalog; it is a provider fulfillment identity, not a customer charge owner.
Workspace-owned persistent storage fulfillment for one Workspace entitlement; it can exist without compute and attach to one active ComputeAllocation at a time.
Mounts one StorageVolume to one ComputeAllocation runtime.

Stable URL subject backed by one persistent StorageVolume, managed runtime login credentials, access state, lifecycle state, and a current replaceable ComputeAllocation/StorageAttachment runtime pointer.
Read-only live USD balance projection sourced by Control Plane from the mapped Sub2API user.
Append-only billing, execution, review, and reconciliation evidence without spendable balance ownership.
Provider execution fact for compute, storage, attachment, runtime, and replayable resource state.
Admin/operator action fact with actor, target, result, IP, user agent, before/after, and timestamps.
Mapping from OPL account/user/workspace/resource context to external support ticket identity.
Control Plane-owned Pilot-wide plain-text announcement with draft, scheduled, published, withdrawn, and per-user read state.
